dependencies: Contracts with approximately 5,700 independent small businesses to conduct certain linehaul and pickup-and-delivery operations
Third-party service providers for information technology infrastructure, application development, maintenance and support
Service providers for aircraft maintenance under third-party service agreements
Global Service Participants for international deliveries
risks: Failure of third-party service providers to perform as expected could have material adverse effect
Disruptions in relationships with service providers or their provision of services
Challenges to employment status of drivers employed by service providers
Difficulties experienced by companies contracted to fly smaller regional feeder aircraft in attracting and retaining pilots
Disruptions in global supply chains limiting access to vehicles and other key capital resources
Service providers may seek to increase financial rates or modify contract terms and may refuse to provide service

regulatory_risks: FAA regulatory authority over aircraft standards, maintenance, personnel and ground facilities
DOT regulatory authority over economic aspects of air transportation and route authority
TSA security requirements under Full All-Cargo Aircraft Operator Standard Security Plan
State regulation over safety and insurance for ground transportation
International aviation regulations and bilateral agreements required for foreign operations
Foreign postal regulations and licensing requirements restricting foreign-owned companies
ICAO Carbon Offsetting and Reduction Scheme for International Aviation (CORSIA)
EU and UK Emissions Trading Scheme (ETS) for aviation
ICAO carbon dioxide emissions standards for aircraft
EU RefuelEU regulation requiring minimum percentage of sustainable aviation fuel
EPA regulation designating certain PFAS as hazardous substances under CERCLA
State and federal laws restricting use of PFAS in firefighting foams
California Air Resources Board Advanced Clean Fleets rule for zero emission trucks
Export control regulations including ECRA and IEEPA with strict liability for carriers
U.S. Customs and Border Protection regulation of customs clearance and brokerage activities
Railway Labor Act (RLA) coverage potentially challenged in connection with Network 2.0
National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) governing labor relations at non-Federal Express subsidiaries
Potential Congressional changes to labor laws making unionization easier
NMB and NLRB actions potentially making employee organization easier
Changes to federal or state laws governing employee classification affecting service provider status
Proposed pilot flight and duty time regulations
Data protection laws including EU GDPR, California Privacy Rights Act, Virginia Consumer Data Protection Act
Artificial intelligence regulations including EU AI Act and Colorado AI Act
China and other jurisdictions' data localization requirements
SEC climate-related disclosure rules (currently stayed)
Environmental regulations on greenhouse gas emissions
Pillar Two global minimum tax framework adopted by multiple countries
Complex and evolving tax laws in U.S. and foreign jurisdictions
Increased tariffs and export controls affecting international trade
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and similar anti-bribery laws in non-U.S. jurisdictions

sanction_risks: Economic sanctions, embargoes, and export controls potentially reducing volume of goods transported globally
U.S. government actions limiting ability of domestic companies to engage in commerce with certain foreign entities
Foreign governments may investigate compliance with U.S. restrictions and take retaliatory action
Sanctions against oil-producing countries or industry participants affecting fuel supply
Services in Ukraine remain limited, services in Belarus remain suspended, exited operations in Russia
Ongoing conflicts between Russia and Ukraine and in the Middle East may lead to further sanctions, airspace bans, regional instability
U.S. executive order relating to sharing information with China and other covered countries
Potential for foreign governments to limit ability of foreign entities to do business with FedEx
